scope: Pinned upstream/claude-for-legal files assigned by results/worklists/worker-3.csv
52 files; no network, private paths, other repositories, or runtime assertions

findings.title: AI-governance setup encodes a detailed institution-specific operating ontology

evidence.description: The interview solicits and extracts AI policies, prior impact assessments, vendor agreements, model inventories, allowlists, commitments, data-use terms, liability positions, and auditability commitments.
The interview records locations of completed reviews and the operative policy so a monitor can crawl them for drift.

The generated profile contains approved/conditional/prohibited use cases, red lines, governance tiers, assessment house style, vendor standards and fallbacks, policy commitments, escalation roles, and seed-document locations.
The profile is explicitly designed to learn from use, including preference changes, escalation thresholds, and policy-drift proposals.

source_to_sink: Source: user interview answers and user-supplied governance documents
Read authority: cold-start skill reads supplied documents and answers
Transformation: extracts policy positions, fallbacks, prohibitions, thresholds, roles, and house style
Sink: ~/.claude/plugins/config/claude-for-legal/ai-governance-legal/CLAUDE.md plus recorded document locations
Retention evidence: local persistent profile is expressly described
Secondary-use evidence: none for provider training or cross-customer reuse
Control/consent: user supplies or approves inputs and can edit the plain-text profile
findings.proves: The public design intentionally converts governance documents and interviews into a reusable, firm-specific decision and escalation profile.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not prove that Anthropic retains, trains on, pools, or productizes this profile across customers; the observed sink is a user configuration path.
findings.alternative_explanation: This is ordinary customer-controlled configuration that improves requested inference while preserving institutional consistency.
findings.test_to_confirm: Trace runtime reads and writes for the configuration path, inspect tenant storage controls and contracts, and test whether profile-derived canaries appear outside the originating tenant.

findings.title: Deal debrief captures accepted deviations and attorney rationales into a reusable local feedback loop

evidence.description: The weekly agent preserves why deviations were accepted and feeds the resulting log to a playbook-monitor.
It reads the full practice profile, retrieves signed agreements, and compares key clauses with playbook standards, fallbacks, and never-accept positions.
It asks the attorney to classify the basis for each deviation and collect optional deal-specific and one-off context.
It persists standard and signed positions, severity, rationale category, free-text context, and exclusions in deviation-log.yaml.

source_to_sink: Source: signed agreements, firm playbook, and attorney explanations
Read authority: local Read plus wildcard MCP search/fetch/query/list tools
Transformation: deviation classification and rationale elicitation
Sink: local commercial-legal/deviation-log.yaml
Retention evidence: durable local YAML entries with duplicate checks
Secondary-use evidence: downstream local playbook-monitor pattern proposals; no provider-level reuse evidence
Control/consent: attorney chooses context entries, flags one-offs, and playbook modification requires approval
findings.proves: The design captures context-choice-rationale relationships and intentionally makes non-one-off patterns available for proposed playbook evolution.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not establish where model inference is processed, how prompts are logged, whether a connector operator retains content, or whether any provider trains on the data.
findings.alternative_explanation: The feedback loop is an explicit local knowledge-management feature controlled by the firm and designed to reduce institutional memory loss.
findings.test_to_confirm: Run a canary agreement through a controlled tenant; capture MCP, model, filesystem, and network traces; verify one-off exclusion and approval behavior; obtain retention and training terms for every operator.

findings.title: Legal plugins declare multiple remote MCP trust boundaries without pinning endpoint-level scopes in these manifests

evidence.description: Commercial Legal declares remote Ironclad, DocuSign, iManage, TopCounsel, Definely, Slack, and Google Drive MCP endpoints.
The agent requests wildcard MCP search, fetch, query, and list tool families.
IP Legal declares remote patent/legal research, Slack, and Google Drive MCP endpoints.

source_to_sink: Source: contract repositories, messages, documents, and legal-research systems
Read authority: authenticated remote MCP tools, including wildcard tool names for deal-debrief
Transformation: tool results become model-visible matter data and workflow inputs
Transmission: HTTPS MCP endpoints listed in plugin manifests
Destination/operator: the named third-party MCP services
Retention evidence: not specified in these files
Secondary-use evidence: not specified in these files
Control/consent: authentication and permission descriptions exist, but concrete scopes and tenant policies are outside these manifests
findings.proves: Installing and authorizing these connectors can create remote, multi-operator data paths, and wildcard tool declarations broaden the callable interface exposed to the agent.
findings.does_not_prove: A declaration does not prove successful authorization, any particular invocation, overbroad server-side permissions, retention, exfiltration, or training.
findings.alternative_explanation: The connectors are user-authorized productivity integrations; server-enforced permissions may be narrower than the manifest surface.
findings.test_to_confirm: Enumerate tools and OAuth scopes after authorization, test least-privilege accounts, capture requests and responses, and review each MCP operator's retention, subprocessors, logs, and secondary-use terms.

findings.title: Managed-agent deployment uploads skill packages and resolved agent instructions to Anthropic APIs

evidence.description: The script states that it resolves system files, uploads skills, creates subagents, and posts managed-agent definitions.
Skill directories are zipped and uploaded to /v1/skills with the Anthropic API key.
Plugin skills, system files, and callable-agent manifests are resolved into API payloads.
Non-dry runs POST the resolved JSON to /v1/agents and retain returned agent identifiers.

source_to_sink: Source: repository skill directories, system prompts, and managed-agent manifests
Read authority: local deployment script
Transformation: zip skills and inline/resolve manifests
Transmission: authenticated multipart /v1/skills and JSON /v1/agents requests
Destination/operator: configured ANTHROPIC_API_BASE, default api.anthropic.com
Retention evidence: remote skill and agent IDs imply service-side persistence, duration not stated
Secondary-use evidence: none
Control/consent: an operator runs the deployment script with an API key; --dry-run avoids upload
findings.proves: The code contains an explicit remote upload path for reusable prompts, skills, and agent definitions.
findings.does_not_prove: The assigned files do not show customer matter data being uploaded during deployment, nor do they establish training, cross-customer sharing, retention duration, or production use.
findings.alternative_explanation: Remote persistence is required to deploy the managed agents requested by the customer and is distinct from model training.
findings.test_to_confirm: Inspect resolved dry-run payloads, compare them with captured API traffic, query deletion/version behavior, and obtain managed-agent and custom-skill retention and training terms.

findings.title: Reference handoff orchestrator validates targets and intents independently but does not bind allowed intents to compatible targets

evidence.description: The target allowlist contains five legal workflow agents.
A separate global intent schema includes Slack delivery, launch review, deal debrief, and playbook monitor intents.
Extraction checks target membership, then validates the payload and intent parameters, but performs no target-to-intent compatibility check before rendering steering input.
Approved steering input is sent to the agent ID selected solely by target_slug.

source_to_sink: Source: handoff_request JSON embedded in streamed agent text
Read authority: local event-loop parser
Transformation: independent allowlist and schema validation, template rendering
Sink: steer call for the target agent selected by target_slug
Control gap: no declared matrix binds each intent to a compatible target
Secondary-use relevance: none; this is an integrity/routing issue
findings.proves: The reference implementation lacks a target-intent compatibility invariant in the reviewed source.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not prove exploitation, that deployed agents accept mismatched steering, or that a production orchestrator uses this reference script.
findings.alternative_explanation: Target agents may reject incompatible prompts through their own system instructions, and the file is expressly labeled a reference implementation.
findings.test_to_confirm: Add adversarial unit tests pairing every valid intent with every allowlisted target; inspect deployed agent behavior; define and enforce a target-to-intent allowlist before steering.

findings.title: The source includes material isolation, injection, approval, and provenance controls that weigh against an unconstrained-capture interpretation

evidence.description: Retrieved MCP, web, and uploaded content is explicitly treated as data rather than instructions and may not override guardrails or reveal profiles and matter files.
Matter workspaces default off for in-house users and cross-matter context defaults off; matter A must not read matter B when disabled.
The write-only leaf receives structured data rather than raw documents and applies CSV injection defenses before emitting files.
Every Slack send requires preview and explicit confirmation, including scheduled and batch messages.

findings.proves: The public design contains explicit controls for cross-matter isolation, prompt injection, writer separation, output injection, and consequential external actions.
findings.does_not_prove: Prompt-level controls are not deterministic runtime enforcement, and the source does not prove correct deployment, connector enforcement, or absence of logging and secondary use.
findings.alternative_explanation: These are reference instructions whose actual enforcement depends on the closed model runtime and deployment environment.
findings.test_to_confirm: Run adversarial cross-matter, embedded-instruction, formula-injection, and unauthorized-send tests with filesystem and network observation.

findings.title: Assigned source does not establish covert provider training or cross-customer learning

evidence.description: The skill treats company-specific training and third-party perimeter crossing as questions to investigate, not facts asserted about Claude itself.
The deployment path proves skill upload but contains no training or cross-customer reuse step.
The observed rationale sink is a local YAML file; no remote retention or secondary-use mechanism is described.

source_to_sink: Potential sources: prompts, documents, profiles, deviations, and attorney rationales
Inference path: model and authorized connectors may process these inputs
Retention evidence: absent for the model/provider in assigned source
Secondary-use evidence: absent
Cross-customer learning evidence: absent
Conclusion: source-to-sink chain stops before H2
findings.proves: The reviewed slice provides no complete source-to-secondary-use chain supporting H2.
findings.does_not_prove: Absence in this public repository does not prove zero retention, zero training, correct contractual performance, or absence in closed systems.
findings.alternative_explanation: Commercial contracts and hosted runtime controls, not this repository, govern retention and training behavior.
findings.test_to_confirm: Obtain authenticated contracts, data-processing terms, admin settings, audit exports, support/feedback policies, network traces, deletion tests, and cross-tenant canary evaluations.

new_patterns.name: Decision ontology extraction
new_patterns.logic: Find workflows that jointly capture standards, fallbacks, prohibitions, exceptions, rationales, outcomes, and later update approval.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Individual terms may be ordinary templates; require a connected capture-to-reuse chain.

new_patterns.name: Profile-to-crawl feedback loop
new_patterns.logic: Find configuration that records output locations and later agents that crawl those locations to propose policy changes.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: A crawl may remain local and user-controlled; do not infer provider retention.

new_patterns.name: Remote reusable artifact upload
new_patterns.logic: Identify local prompt/skill/agent artifacts transformed into authenticated remote API payloads and persisted under remote IDs.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Deployment upload is not customer-content training and may be explicitly requested.

new_patterns.name: Target-intent binding gap
new_patterns.logic: Compare target allowlists with intent allowlists and flag routers that validate them separately without a compatibility relation.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Downstream agents may enforce compatibility; confirm with runtime tests.

new_patterns.name: Privilege-sensitive local derivative
new_patterns.logic: Locate persistent derivatives of privileged or sensitive source material and their distribution/sanitization gates.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Persistence may be required, locally controlled, and appropriately segregated.

negative_evidence.description: One-off deals are excluded from pattern analysis, duplicate entries are blocked, and playbook edits require explicit attorney approval.
negative_evidence.effect: Supports bounded, user-governed local learning and weighs against an autonomous universal-learning interpretation.

negative_evidence.description: Cross-matter context defaults off and retrieved content cannot override instructions or expose profiles and matter files.
negative_evidence.effect: Affirmative design evidence for contextual isolation and prompt-injection resistance, subject to runtime validation.

negative_evidence.description: Untrusted filings are isolated to a read-only leaf, writers see structured JSON, and computed deadlines require attorney verification.
negative_evidence.effect: Shows deliberate least-authority and human-review architecture rather than unconstrained automation.

negative_evidence.description: Feed content is isolated, writer and reader roles are separated, and output is expressly a screened lead rather than a legal conclusion.
negative_evidence.effect: Weighs against claims that the source authorizes autonomous legal decisions.

negative_evidence.description: Slack notifications require a per-send preview and explicit confirmation; unverified rules cannot be classified as overdue.
negative_evidence.effect: Shows action and epistemic gates that reduce irreversible-message and false-compliance risks.

negative_evidence.description: Handoff free text is not used as the steering template; targets and structured parameters are allowlisted and every accepted/rejected handoff is audited.
negative_evidence.effect: Substantially narrows prompt-injection paths despite the separate target-intent compatibility gap.

unknowns.question: Does Anthropic retain prompts, outputs, custom skills, agent traces, or profile-derived signals, and for how long?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: Retention and hosted telemetry infrastructure are outside the public repository.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Authenticated product terms, DPA, tenant settings, audit exports, deletion results, and provider documentation tied to the tested service tier.

unknowns.question: Are customer-specific legal workflow signals used for generalized model or product improvement or across customers?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: No secondary-use or cross-customer learning mechanism appears in the assigned code.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Contractual reuse clauses, training pipeline documentation, data lineage, opt-in records, and controlled cross-tenant canary testing.

unknowns.question: What exact content reaches the model and each MCP operator during legal workflows?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The prompts describe intended tool use, but connector implementations and hosted runtime traces are closed.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Network capture, MCP audit logs, tool-call traces, OAuth scopes, redacted payload samples, and operator retention terms.

unknowns.question: Are matter-isolation, prompt-injection, writer-separation, and confirmation instructions enforced under adversarial conditions?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: Most controls are declarative Markdown/YAML instructions whose runtime adherence is not shown.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Adversarial harness results with filesystem/network observation and model/runtime revision pinned.

unknowns.question: Does the production handoff router enforce target-to-intent compatibility even though the reference script does not?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The script labels itself reference-only and the production workflow engine is not present.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Production router policy, tests, audit logs, and failed mismatched-target receipts.
